title: QuickTime Video
documentation:
## Purpose
MOV is Apple's QuickTime container format, which organizes video, audio, and metadata into a track-based structure. It is the native output format of many Apple devices, Final Cut Pro, and other professional macOS production tools.

## When to use
Use [QuickTime Video](https://capdag.com/browse/media:mov;video) when working with content originating from Apple cameras (iPhone, iPad), macOS screen recordings, or professional editing pipelines that target Final Cut Pro or Motion. MOV files often carry high-quality ProRes or H.264 video and are a natural intermediate format in Apple-centric workflows.

## Notes
MOV and MP4 share the ISO Base Media File Format heritage and are structurally similar, but MOV supports certain Apple-specific metadata atoms not found in standard MP4. On non-Apple platforms, playback and remuxing support varies by tool; most modern FFmpeg-based pipelines handle MOV without issue.
